text:
Once upon a time in sleepy Willow Creek there lived an old wise owl named Oliver. Oliver loved fishing at the crystal-clear pond near his cozy owl nest. Despite his age he remained fit thanks to regular swim lessons with his dear friend Ducklings. One serene afternoon Oliver decided it was time to invent something.

He imagined threading fisherman lines with beads woven of friendship. Ducklings thought they sounded silly. But Oliver loved playing tricks. That night he slept dreaming of lantern sails gliding softly upon the water's coat.

The next morning Oliver crafted them. Ducklings thought they were ridiculous. Oliver merely laughed and hid them in leaves. On his first fishing trip, he cast his enchanted lines. Fish danced around and then he captured them. Laughter echoed through the trees while squirrels pointed and giggled.

Word spread; villagers chuckled and caught their own fish. Oliver became famous. Fish knew it was good luck. Birds carried tokens. Children left little bells.

In time Willow Creek flourished. Oliver
